stories.headline: Europe Today: Zelenskyy, E3 leaders agree peace terms as pro-EU party claims victory in Armenia
stories.summary: The leaders of Germany, France, the UK and Ukraine agreed on concrete conditions for peace yesterday, including Europe's participation in direct talks with Vladimir Putin. In Armenia, a pro-EU party has claimed victory in the elections held yesterday.

stories.headline: Powerful earthquake in southern Philippines leaves at least 19 dead

stories.headline: Korean Leveraged ETF Misfires, Jumps 50% Even as SK Hynix Slumps

stories.summary: Fear was already running high ahead of South Korea’s Monday open, but the ferocity of the selloff still caught some investors off guard.

stories.headline: Universal Music Sounds Out Investors for €1 Billion Bond Sale
stories.summary: Universal Music Group NV is speaking to investors ahead of a possible €1 billion ($1.15 billion) two-part bond sale on Monday, after rejecting hedge fund billionaire Bill Ackman’s takeover offer.

stories.headline: Blackstone Looks to Sell $2 Billion of Fund Stakes, FT Says
stories.summary: Blackstone Inc. is looking to sell more than $2 billion of its stakes in private investment funds by bundling the exposure into bonds for investors, the Financial Times reported.

stories.headline: Wall Street Strategists See Rally Intact After Healthy Tech Drop
stories.summary: Strategists at some of Wall Street’s biggest banks are looking past Friday’s tech-led selloff, saying earnings growth should support stocks through the end of the year.

stories.headline: M&A Monday: Intesa's €31 Billion Bid for Paschi & Tate & Lyle Takeover | The Opening Trade 6/8/2026
stories.summary:
Intesa Sanpaolo offered to buy Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena for €30.6 billion in a move that’s set to spur a new phase of dealmaking in Italian finance. It comes a day after Banco BPM pitched a merger of equals with Monte Paschi that would result in a combined entity worth close to €50 billion.

stories.headline: Tencent Aims to Raise $3 Billion in Dollar, Yuan Bond Offering
stories.summary: Tencent Holdings Ltd. is preparing to raise around $3 billion in a dual-currency bond offering that could be priced as early as Tuesday, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Chile’s Tren de Aragua Probe Revives Bank Secrecy Debate
stories.summary: The June arrest of a bank employee accused of ties to Venezuela’s Tren de Aragua criminal organization has reignited debate over Chile’s financial secrecy rules, which are among the strictest in the world.

stories.headline: Schroders Shuns Treasuries and Bunds in Favor of Italian Debt
stories.summary: Asset manager Schroders is buying Italian government bonds, arguing the country has already weathered the budget and political turmoil now rattling investors in other European nations and the US.

stories.headline: Record Fine Hits Romanian Bank Stocks After Interbank Rate Probe
stories.summary: Romania’s largest bank shares tumbled after the nation’s competition watchdog imposed a record fine over alleged collusion in setting money-market rates.

stories.headline: China Ramping Up LNG Buying in Anticipation of Summer Heat

stories.headline: Private Markets Elite Gather in Berlin Amid Not-So-Super Returns
stories.summary: Private market dealmakers attending SuperReturn in Berlin this week are likely to be handed a short but sharp to-do list by their investors: give us our money back.

stories.headline: Tate & Lyle Accepts Bid From Ingredion in Loss for London
stories.summary: Ingredion Inc. agreed to buy Tate & Lyle Plc for £2.7 billion ($3.6 billion), in a move that marks the end of the UK company’s near-century on the London Stock Exchange.

stories.headline: India Rupee Defense Lifts Key Forex Tool Past $110 Billion Mark
stories.summary: The Reserve Bank of India’s use of a key tool for defending the rupee has passed the $110 billion mark in recent weeks to a new record, according to people familiar with the developments.

stories.headline: In Maine, Supporters of Graham Platner Continue to Back His Senate Campaign, With ‘Trepidation’
stories.summary: Many are sticking by the presumptive Democratic Senate nominee Graham Platner. But some have soured, and others are anxious about how recent revelations could affect a close race.

stories.headline: Robert Coles, Pulitzer-Winning Child Psychiatrist, Is Dead at 97
stories.summary: His five-volume “Children of Crisis” series, published between 1967 and 1977, drew on his conversations with American children whose voices were not often heard.
